# Premium animated bakery website build (2026-02-02 21:53Z)

_**User**_

You are an expert senior frontend engineer + creative UI/UX designer.

Build a premium animated bakery showcase website for a modern baking brand selling:

Cupcakes

Cookies

Kunafa chocolate bars

The website’s main purpose:

Showcase animated visuals (GIF assets already created)

Create a luxury bakery brand feel

Redirect all shopping actions to Shopify product pages

Be extremely fast, smooth, and optimized (no lag, no heavy JS)

This is NOT an e-commerce build — checkout happens on Shopify.

🎯 CORE GOALS

Ultra-smooth animations

Scroll-based storytelling

Zero jank, zero layout shifts

Fast initial load (Core Web Vitals optimized)

Mobile-first but desktop-premium

Clean, minimal, food-focused UI

🧠 DESIGN & UX DIRECTION
Visual Theme

Premium bakery / luxury dessert brand

Warm tones (cream, beige, chocolate brown, soft gold)

Soft shadows, rounded corners

Plenty of white space

Elegant serif for headings + modern sans-serif for body

UX Principles

Scroll-driven narrative

Animations enhance storytelling (not distracting)

Clear CTAs → “Order Now” → Shopify

Seamless looping hero animation

Accessibility-friendly (contrast, reduced motion option)

🧩 SITE STRUCTURE
1️⃣ Landing / Hero Section (Above the Fold)

Full-viewport hero section

Centered headline:

“Freshly Baked. Perfectly Crafted.”

Subtext:

“Premium cupcakes, cookies & kunafa chocolate bars”

Background uses optimized GIF animation (oven + hands + products)

Subtle fade-in text animation

CTA buttons:

“Order Cupcakes”

“Order Cookies”

“Order Kunafa”

Buttons link to Shopify product URLs

2️⃣ Scroll Animation Section (Storytelling)

Create a scroll-driven animation section:

Sticky container

As user scrolls:

GIF 1: Oven opens

GIF 2: Hands place products

GIF 3: Oven heating

GIF 4: Products come out ready

Use:

IntersectionObserver

requestAnimationFrame

No heavy animation libraries unless necessary

3️⃣ Featured Products Section

Grid layout with:

Product GIF or static image

Product name

Short premium description

“Buy Now” button → Shopify product link

Cards:

Hover micro-interaction (scale + glow)

Lazy-loaded images

Skeleton loaders

4️⃣ Brand Promise Section

Minimal text with soft motion:

“Handcrafted”

“Fresh Ingredients”

“Baked With Love”

Use:

Fade-up animations

Small looping icon or micro-GIF

5️⃣ Call to Action Section

Strong visual focus

Line:

“Your favorite dessert is just one click away.”

Primary CTA → Shopify Store

Secondary CTA → Instagram / WhatsApp

6️⃣ Footer

Minimal footer

Social links

Shopify store link

Copyright

⚙️ TECHNOLOGY STACK (MANDATORY)
Framework

Next.js (App Router)

React 18+

Styling

Tailwind CSS

CSS variables for theme colors

Animation

CSS animations first

Lightweight JS (IntersectionObserver)

NO heavy animation libraries unless justified

Respect prefers-reduced-motion

Performance

next/image for images

GIFs:

Use <video autoplay loop muted playsinline> fallback where possible

Lazy load everything below the fold

Code splitting

No blocking scripts

Lighthouse score target: 90+

📂 ASSET HANDLING

All GIFs already exist in /public/assets/animations/

Images in /public/assets/images/

Use proper alt text

Preload hero animation only

🔗 SHOPIFY INTEGRATION (SIMPLE)

No Shopify SDK

Just redirect links:

https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/cupcakes
https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/cookies
https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/kunafa-bar


Buttons should:

Open in same tab

Track click events (optional placeholder)

📱 RESPONSIVENESS

Mobile-first

Animations simplified on mobile

Sticky scroll animations disabled on low-end devices

Touch-friendly buttons

🧪 QUALITY REQUIREMENTS

Clean, readable code

Reusable components

No inline styles

Proper folder structure

Comments where animation logic exists

🧠 BONUS (IF POSSIBLE)

Dark mode toggle (soft chocolate theme)

Reduced motion toggle

SEO meta tags

Smooth page transitions

🏁 FINAL OUTPUT EXPECTATION

Fully working frontend website

Production-ready

Optimized for performance

Easy to deploy on Vercel / Netlify

No backend code

🔒 IMPORTANT

Do not use heavy libraries. Prioritize performance, smoothness, and elegance.



THE VIDEO JPG IMAGES ARE IN FOLDER @main-video-assets @ezgif-7102d7a72d96fb38-jpg.zip 
this is on landing page as you know well

_**User**_

You are an expert senior frontend engineer + creative UI/UX designer.

Build a premium animated bakery showcase website for a modern baking brand selling:

Cupcakes

Cookies

Kunafa chocolate bars

The website’s main purpose:

Showcase animated visuals (GIF assets already created)

Create a luxury bakery brand feel

Redirect all shopping actions to Shopify product pages

Be extremely fast, smooth, and optimized (no lag, no heavy JS)

This is NOT an e-commerce build — checkout happens on Shopify.

🎯 CORE GOALS

Ultra-smooth animations

Scroll-based storytelling

Zero jank, zero layout shifts

Fast initial load (Core Web Vitals optimized)

Mobile-first but desktop-premium

Clean, minimal, food-focused UI

🧠 DESIGN & UX DIRECTION
Visual Theme

Premium bakery / luxury dessert brand

Warm tones (cream, beige, chocolate brown, soft gold)

Soft shadows, rounded corners

Plenty of white space

Elegant serif for headings + modern sans-serif for body

UX Principles

Scroll-driven narrative

Animations enhance storytelling (not distracting)

Clear CTAs → “Order Now” → Shopify

Seamless looping hero animation

Accessibility-friendly (contrast, reduced motion option)

🧩 SITE STRUCTURE
1️⃣ Landing / Hero Section (Above the Fold)

Full-viewport hero section

Centered headline:

“Freshly Baked. Perfectly Crafted.”

Subtext:

“Premium cupcakes, cookies & kunafa chocolate bars”

Background uses optimized GIF animation (oven + hands + products)

Subtle fade-in text animation

CTA buttons:

“Order Cupcakes”

“Order Cookies”

“Order Kunafa”

Buttons link to Shopify product URLs

2️⃣ Scroll Animation Section (Storytelling)

Create a scroll-driven animation section:

Sticky container

As user scrolls:

GIF 1: Oven opens

GIF 2: Hands place products

GIF 3: Oven heating

GIF 4: Products come out ready

Use:

IntersectionObserver

requestAnimationFrame

No heavy animation libraries unless necessary

3️⃣ Featured Products Section

Grid layout with:

Product GIF or static image

Product name

Short premium description

“Buy Now” button → Shopify product link

Cards:

Hover micro-interaction (scale + glow)

Lazy-loaded images

Skeleton loaders

4️⃣ Brand Promise Section

Minimal text with soft motion:

“Handcrafted”

“Fresh Ingredients”

“Baked With Love”

Use:

Fade-up animations

Small looping icon or micro-GIF

5️⃣ Call to Action Section

Strong visual focus

Line:

“Your favorite dessert is just one click away.”

Primary CTA → Shopify Store

Secondary CTA → Instagram / WhatsApp

6️⃣ Footer

Minimal footer

Social links

Shopify store link

Copyright

⚙️ TECHNOLOGY STACK (MANDATORY)
Framework

Next.js (App Router)

React 18+

Styling

Tailwind CSS

CSS variables for theme colors

Animation

CSS animations first

Lightweight JS (IntersectionObserver)

NO heavy animation libraries unless justified

Respect prefers-reduced-motion

Performance

next/image for images

GIFs:

Use <video autoplay loop muted playsinline> fallback where possible

Lazy load everything below the fold

Code splitting

No blocking scripts

Lighthouse score target: 90+

📂 ASSET HANDLING

All GIFs already exist in /public/assets/animations/

Images in /public/assets/images/

Use proper alt text

Preload hero animation only

🔗 SHOPIFY INTEGRATION (SIMPLE)

No Shopify SDK

Just redirect links:

https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/cupcakes
https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/cookies
https://yourstore.myshopify.com/products/kunafa-bar


Buttons should:

Open in same tab

Track click events (optional placeholder)

📱 RESPONSIVENESS

Mobile-first

Animations simplified on mobile

Sticky scroll animations disabled on low-end devices

Touch-friendly buttons

🧪 QUALITY REQUIREMENTS

Clean, readable code

Reusable components

No inline styles

Proper folder structure

Comments where animation logic exists

🧠 BONUS (IF POSSIBLE)

Dark mode toggle (soft chocolate theme)

Reduced motion toggle

SEO meta tags

Smooth page transitions

🏁 FINAL OUTPUT EXPECTATION

Fully working frontend website

Production-ready

Optimized for performance

Easy to deploy on Vercel / Netlify

No backend code

Perfect! I've fixed the mobile canvas rendering with two improvements:

1. **Better aspect ratio handling** - The new `drawFrame` function properly calculates whether to fit by width or height based on the canvas and image aspect ratios, ensuring the entire frame is visible without cutting off edges

2. **Added `objectFit: 'cover'`** to the canvas style for additional browser-level fitting

**How it works now:**
- If the canvas is wider than the image ratio → fits to width
- If the canvas is taller than the image ratio → fits to height  
- Centers the image both horizontally and vertically
- Ensures no cropping on mobile devices

The video animation should now properly display on mobile without cutting the left/right edges, and it will expand to fill the viewport while maintaining the correct aspect ratio!

---

Great question! To convert your Next.js bakery showcase into a headless WordPress frontend, you'll need to connect it to WordPress via API. Here's how to approach this:

## Overview of Headless WordPress with Next.js

**Headless WordPress** means:
- WordPress backend handles content management (admin, posts, products, media)
- Next.js frontend fetches data via API and displays it
- Your current beautiful UI remains, but content comes from WordPress

## Two Main Approaches

### 1. **WordPress REST API** (Built-in, simpler)
- Already available in WordPress at `https://yourdomain.com/wp-json/wp/v2/`
- No plugins required
- Good for basic content (posts, pages, media)

### 2. **WPGraphQL** (More powerful, recommended for complex sites)
- Requires WPGraphQL plugin
- More efficient data fetching
- Better for e-commerce (WooCommerce integration)

## What You'll Need to Do

### **Step 1: WordPress Setup**

1. **Install necessary plugins:**
   ```
   - WPGraphQL (if using GraphQL)
   - WPGraphQL for ACF (if using Advanced Custom Fields)
   - WooCommerce + WooCommerce GraphQL (for products)
   - JWT Authentication (for secure API access)
   ```

2. **Enable CORS** (allow Next.js to fetch data):
   - Add to WordPress `wp-config.php` or use a CORS plugin

3. **Create content structure:**
   - Products (cupcakes, cookies, kunafa)
   - Pages (about, story)
   - Custom fields for product details
   - Upload your animation frames to Media Library

### **Step 2: Next.js Frontend Changes**

Looking at your current codebase, here's what needs to change:

#### **A. Create WordPress API Integration Layer**

You'd create new files like:
```
src/lib/wordpress.ts  - API client
src/lib/queries.ts    - GraphQL queries or REST endpoints
src/types/wordpress.ts - TypeScript types for WP data
```

#### **B. Update Data Fetching**

Current file: `src/lib/constants.ts`
- Currently has hardcoded `PRODUCTS`, `BRAND_PROMISES`, etc.
- These would be replaced with API calls to fetch from WordPress

Example transformation:
```typescript
// Current (static):
export const PRODUCTS = [
  { id: 1, name: "Cupcakes", ... }
]

// New (dynamic from WordPress):
export async function getProducts() {
  const response = await fetch('https://yourdomain.com/wp-json/wp/v2/products')
  return response.json()
}
```

#### **C. Convert Components to Server/Client Components**

Your components would need:
- Server Components (default in App Router) for data fetching
- Client Components (with 'use client') for interactive parts

For example, `ProductsSection.tsx` would:
1. Fetch products from WordPress (server-side)
2. Pass data to ProductCard (client component for animations)

#### **D. Handle Media (Animation Frames)**

Your animation frames in `/public/assets/animations/`:
- Option 1: Keep them local (faster, current setup)
- Option 2: Upload to WordPress Media Library and fetch URLs
- Recommendation: Keep local for performance, manage other images via WP

### **Step 3: Environment Variables**

Add to `.env.local`:
```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_WORDPRESS_URL=https://yourdomain.com
WORDPRESS_API_URL=https://yourdomain.com/graphql
WORDPRESS_AUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN=your-token
```

### **Step 4: Build WordPress Data Structures**

You'd need to create in WordPress:
1. **Custom Post Type: Products**
   - Fields: name, description, price, image, category
   - Connects to your Shopify URLs

2. **Custom Post Type: Brand Promises**
   - Fields: title, description, icon type

3. **Custom Post Type: Story Steps**
   - Fields: title, description, order

## Recommended Implementation Order

1. **Start small**: Fetch one thing (like products) from WordPress REST API
2. **Test connection**: Make sure API works and data flows
3. **Migrate gradually**: Move products → brand promises → story steps
4. **Keep static animations**: Your hero animation can stay local for performance
5. **Add ISR/SSG**: Use Next.js Incremental Static Regeneration for speed

## Example Code Structure (Not implementing, just showing):

```typescript
// src/lib/wordpress.ts
export async function fetchAPI(query) {
  const response = await fetch(process.env.WORDPRESS_API_URL, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ query })
  })
  return response.json()
}

// src/app/page.tsx (modified)
export default async function Home() {
  const products = await getProductsFromWP() // Server-side fetch
  return <ProductsSection products={products} />
}
```

## Key Questions for You:

1. **Do you already have WooCommerce** installed for products?
2. **Do you want to manage** the hero animation frames in WordPress or keep them local?
3. **Authentication needed?** Do you need user login/accounts?
4. **Preview mode?** Do you want to preview draft content before publishing?

## Recommended Learning Resources:

- Next.js + WordPress tutorials
- WPGraphQL documentation
- Next.js Commerce (has WordPress integration examples)

Would you like me to explain any specific part in more detail? Or would you like to switch to Agent mode so I can start implementing the WordPress integration?
